_comment: Optional user personalization for the Soniox v4 engines. Copy this file to 'personal_settings.json' and customize the vocabulary block. If 'personal_settings.json' is absent, SONIOX_CONTEXT stays None and no personalization is sent. Only used by the Soniox Live transcriber and the v4 path of the Soniox upload slot (long recordings / V2-failure fallback) — the short-recording v2 sync path and the Groq APIs ignore it.

general.value: Your main topics here (e.g. software development, academic writing, specific field)

terms: Technical term the model often misrecognizes
Foreign word commonly used in your dictation

push_to_talk._comment: Opt-in push-to-talk (Issue #66): tap the trigger key, release, then press-and-HOLD it; recording runs while held, releasing inserts the transcript. DEFAULT OFF -- set enabled to true to use it (the gesture then reads every trigger press). On German QWERTZ, AltGr (Ctrl+Alt) is filtered out, so typing @ \ { } [ ] | euro ~ never triggers it. trigger: lctrl (default, bottom-left) | rctrl (AltGr-safe) | lalt. insert: type (default, simulated typing like Ctrl+Alt+A) | clipboard | send | no_insert. The three thresholds are seconds (positive numbers): tap_window_s = first release to second press, min_hold_s = how long the second press is held before recording starts, release_tail_s = extra time kept after release so word-ends are not clipped.
